methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Scout AI is a seed-stage defense and security software company founded by Colby Adcock and Collin Otis that has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (Stark Defence at $500M Series A, Vatn Systems at $300M Series A, Ares Industries at $150M Series A, and Tenna Systems at $60M Seed), alongside two public comparables and a broader universe of eight private frontier-tech peers in defense applications.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses Scout AI's $15M raised as a value proxy, applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $38M. The peer-median approach references the four sampled Defense & Security companies at equivalent or adjacent stages, producing a $225M implied valuation with a range of $60M to $500M. The substantial dispersion reflects the heterogeneity of Series A outcomes in this sector.

Reconciling both methods produces a triangulated range of $32M to $259M, with a median of $131M. The wide band reflects meaningful uncertainty in early-stage defense tech valuations, where comparable multiples and stage-step assumptions vary significantly. Key uncertainties include the specific timing and stage of Scout AI's next funding round relative to peer precedent, and whether the company's technology and market position align more closely with earlier-stage or later-stage comparable outcomes within the peer set.
